wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the AI Editor Engine project overview. Explain the purpose as a framework-agnostic design tool engine for creating educational slide content, its target audience of developers building design tools, and core philosophy of separating concerns between data modeling, rendering, and interactive capabilities. Document the project's educational focus on Slides Editor implementation, architectural principles emphasizing framework agnosticism, and relationship to modern design tool patterns. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners understanding design tool architecture and technical details for experienced developers implementing similar systems. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including scene graph, command pattern, and timeline engine. Provide practical examples demonstrating the separation of engine, renderer, and store layers.

wiki_catalogs.name: DOM Rendering Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the DOM rendering implementation that transforms scene graph data into HTML elements. Document the DOM rendering functions, element creation and manipulation processes, CSS property application for positioning, sizing, and styling. Explain how the renderer maps scene graph coordinates to DOM positions, handles element hierarchy and layer ordering, and manages visual feedback during user interactions. Include examples of rendering different element types (shapes, images, text), CSS transform applications for rotation and scaling, and performance optimization techniques for large scenes. Address coordinate system transformations, viewport management, and how the DOM renderer integrates with React components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layered Architecture Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the layered architecture design of the AI Editor Engine. Explain the three-layer separation: engine (framework-agnostic core logic), renderer (data-to-UI transformation), and store (editor state management). Document how each layer maintains clear responsibilities and communicates with adjacent layers. Include architectural diagrams showing data flow from user input through engine execution to DOM updates. Explain the benefits of this separation including testability, maintainability, and framework independence. Address how the command pattern enforces single source of truth and how plugins integrate across layers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Engine Class Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Engine class implementation that serves as the central command execution hub. Document the singleton pattern enforcement, framework-agnostic design principles, and the core execute method that validates and processes all commands. Explain the engine initialization process, plugin registration mechanism, and how the engine maintains the single source of truth for all state modifications. Include practical examples of engine instantiation, command execution patterns, and error handling strategies. Address the relationship between engine operations and scene graph updates, performance considerations for command processing, and integration patterns with external systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Timeline Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Timeline Engine that manages time-based animation playback. Document the timeline architecture, time progression mechanisms, and animation scheduling system. Explain how the timeline coordinates multiple animations, handles time-based interpolation, and maintains synchronization across different animation types. Include examples of timeline initialization, time control methods, and animation queuing. Address performance optimization for smooth playback, memory management for animation states, and integration with the render loop. Document timeline events, callback mechanisms, and debugging approaches for timing-sensitive animations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the AI Editor Engine. Cover prerequisites including Node.js version requirements, installation process using npm/yarn, development server startup with Vite, and initial project structure walkthrough. Document environment setup, build processes, and basic usage patterns. Include step-by-step instructions for running the development server, accessing the editor interface, and understanding the basic workflow. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues and explain the relationship between different configuration files. Make content accessible to developers new to the project while ensuring they can quickly get productive with the editor engine.

wiki_catalogs.name: Transform Calculations and Coordinate Systems
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for transform calculations and coordinate system management in the rendering pipeline. Document the mathematical transformations for positioning, scaling, rotation, and skew operations applied to elements. Explain coordinate system conversions between scene graph space and screen space, pivot point calculations for transformations, and matrix operations for complex transformations. Include examples of transform composition, handling parent-child coordinate relationships, and managing transformation inheritance in the scene graph. Address performance considerations for transform calculations, precision handling for floating-point operations, and optimization techniques for batch rendering scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Interactions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for component interactions within the AI Editor Engine architecture. Document how Canvas components integrate with the engine through command execution, how the renderer transforms scene data into UI components, and how the store manages editor state separate from scene data. Explain the observer pattern implementation for timeline-driven animations and how components subscribe to state changes. Include sequence diagrams showing typical user interaction flows from drag operations to final DOM updates. Address event propagation, callback mechanisms, and error handling across component boundaries.

wiki_catalogs.name: Scene Graph Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the scene graph architecture that manages hierarchical relationships between slides, elements, and animations. Detail the document structure containing multiple slides, slide hierarchy with elementIds arrays, and element composition including position, size, rotation, and z-index properties. Explain the parent-child relationships, coordinate system transformations, and how the scene graph supports drag-and-drop operations, grouping, and layer management. Include practical examples of scene graph traversal, element manipulation, and hierarchical updates. Document the data structures for different element types (shapes, images, text, groups), animation inheritance patterns, and performance optimization techniques for large scene graphs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Keyframe Interpolation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the Keyframe Interpolation system that calculates intermediate animation states. Document the mathematical algorithms for linear, ease-in, ease-out, and custom easing functions. Explain keyframe storage, interpolation calculation methods, and transform matrix computation. Include examples of setting up keyframes for position, rotation, scale, and opacity animations. Address spline interpolation for smooth curves, keyframe blending techniques, and performance optimization for complex interpolation calculations. Document interpolation precision, numerical stability, and debugging tools for animation timing issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the AI Editor Engine system architecture. Describe the layered architecture with clear separation between engine (core logic), renderer (data-to-UI transformation), and store (editor state management). Document component interactions, data flows from user input through engine execution to DOM updates, and integration patterns between layers. Explain technical decisions behind framework-agnostic design, trade-offs between performance and flexibility, and constraints imposed by the command pattern system. Include system context diagrams showing how Canvas components, timeline engine, and plugin system interact. Address cross-cutting concerns like state management, animation coordination, and undo/redo functionality. Document technology stack including React, TypeScript, and Vite integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layer Management and Visual Ordering
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for layer management and visual ordering in the rendering system. Document how z-index values are processed and applied to control element stacking order, the layer sorting algorithm for efficient rendering, and handling of overlapping elements. Explain the relationship between scene graph hierarchy and visual layering, managing element visibility and opacity effects, and implementing layer-specific styling and effects. Include examples of layer manipulation, handling complex layer hierarchies, and optimizing render performance through efficient layer sorting and culling techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for data flow patterns throughout the AI Editor Engine system. Document the unidirectional data flow from user interactions through engine.execute() to state updates and UI rendering. Explain how scene graph data flows through the renderer to produce UI components, and how editor state flows through the store to drive UI behavior. Include examples of typical data transformations at each layer, such as coordinate transformations, animation keyframe interpolation, and property serialization. Address performance considerations for large datasets and optimization strategies for smooth user interactions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Command Pattern System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the command pattern system that enables undo/redo functionality and centralized state management. Document the command interface design, execution flow, and serialization mechanisms. Explain how commands encapsulate state changes, support reversible operations, and maintain consistency across the scene graph. Include practical examples of command creation, execution patterns, and history management. Detail the command registry system, parameter validation, and error rollback mechanisms. Address performance considerations for command storage, memory management for history stacks, and integration with the timeline system for animation commands.

wiki_catalogs.name: Animation Playback Control
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Animation Playback Control that manages animation execution states and user interaction. Document play/pause/stop mechanisms, scrubbing controls, and frame-by-frame playback. Explain animation state management, progress tracking, and synchronization with user-triggered animations (auto/click). Include examples of programmatic animation control, event handling for playback events, and integration with the timeline system. Address animation looping, reverse playback, speed control, and performance considerations for real-time playback. Document animation state persistence, recovery mechanisms, and debugging approaches for playback synchronization issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Engine System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Core Engine System that serves as the central command execution hub. Detail the Engine class implementation, scene graph architecture for hierarchical slide and element management, command pattern system enabling undo/redo functionality, and history management mechanisms. Document the framework-agnostic design principles, singleton pattern enforcement, and how all state modifications must go through engine.execute(command). Explain the relationship between engine operations and scene graph updates, command serialization/deserialization, and plugin integration points. Include practical examples of command execution, scene graph traversal, and state mutation patterns. Address performance considerations, memory management, and error handling strategies within the engine layer.

wiki_catalogs.name: Canvas Renderer Integration Plans
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for future canvas renderer integration plans and framework-agnostic design considerations. Document the planned transition from DOM-based rendering to canvas-based rendering, the abstract renderer interface design that enables multiple backend implementations, and migration strategies for existing DOM rendering code. Explain the benefits of canvas rendering for performance optimization, advanced graphics capabilities, and scalability improvements. Include architectural patterns for renderer abstraction, plugin system for renderer selection, and compatibility considerations for maintaining backward compatibility during the transition phase.

wiki_catalogs.name: Design Patterns and Principles
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive content for the design patterns and architectural principles underlying the AI Editor Engine. Document the command pattern implementation for state management, observer pattern for timeline animations, factory pattern for engine instantiation, and plugin architecture for extensibility. Explain how these patterns work together to achieve framework-agnostic design and maintainable code structure. Include concrete examples from the codebase showing pattern implementations, their benefits, and trade-offs. Address how these patterns enable features like undo/redo, animation playback, and plugin system integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: History Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the history management system that tracks command execution and enables undo/redo functionality. Detail the history stack implementation, command serialization for persistence, and state snapshot mechanisms. Explain the relationship between command execution and history updates, memory management for large histories, and performance optimization strategies. Include practical examples of undo/redo operations, history navigation, and command filtering. Document the integration with the command pattern system, conflict resolution for concurrent modifications, and restoration of previous states. Address limitations, memory considerations, and best practices for managing history in collaborative environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Path Animation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for Path Animation that enables complex motion along bezier curves and custom paths. Document path definition systems, control point manipulation, and tangent calculation for smooth movement. Explain path parameterization, arc-length calculation, and velocity control along curved trajectories. Include examples of creating circular, elliptical, and custom bezier paths. Address path optimization, collision detection, and integration with element transforms. Document path animation performance considerations, memory usage for complex paths, and debugging tools for path construction and animation issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Rendering System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Rendering System that transforms scene graph data into visual UI components. Document the renderer layer architecture as pure data-to-UI transformation utilities independent of React lifecycle, DOM rendering implementation with precise coordinate mapping, and transform application for positioning, scaling, and rotation. Explain the relationship between renderer functions and React components, coordinate system transformations, and performance optimization techniques. Include examples of rendering different element types (shapes, images, text), handling layer ordering, and managing visual feedback during user interactions. Address future plans for canvas renderer integration and how the current DOM-based approach can be extended to support alternative rendering targets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Plugin Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the plugin architecture that extends engine functionality through standardized integration points. Document the plugin registration mechanism, lifecycle management, and API contracts for component plugins, panel plugins, command plugins, and shortcut plugins. Explain the plugin loading system, dependency injection patterns, and how plugins interact with the core engine without violating framework-agnostic principles. Include practical examples of plugin development, registration patterns, and integration with existing systems. Detail the plugin isolation mechanisms, error handling strategies, and performance considerations for plugin execution. Address plugin versioning, compatibility management, and best practices for plugin development.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the State Management system that handles editor state separate from scene data. Document the Zustand store architecture, separation between editor state (selection, zoom, tools) and scene data (slides, elements), and state synchronization mechanisms. Explain how the store integrates with React components, state persistence strategies, and performance optimization through selective re-rendering. Include examples of state subscriptions, action dispatching, and state reset scenarios. Address the relationship between store state and engine commands, ensuring consistency between user interface state and underlying scene graph data. Document state migration patterns and debugging approaches for complex state interactions.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Interface Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for User Interface Components focusing on the Canvas component implementation and overall layout structure. Document the Canvas component's role in visual editing, drag-and-drop operations, element selection and manipulation, and integration with the engine system. Explain the header and layout structure, responsive design considerations, component styling and theming approaches, and user interaction patterns. Include examples of component props, event handling, and integration with React hooks. Address accessibility considerations, cross-browser compatibility, and performance optimization for smooth user interactions. Document component composition patterns and how UI components communicate with the underlying engine layer.

wiki_catalogs.name: Animation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Animation System that provides timeline-based animation capabilities. Document the timeline engine architecture, keyframe interpolation algorithms, animation playback control, and path animation implementation with control points. Explain how animations are coordinated with the scene graph, transform application over time, and integration with user-triggered animations (auto/click). Include examples of setting up keyframes, configuring animation timing and easing, and previewing animations. Address performance considerations for smooth animation playback, memory management for animation data, and the relationship between animation state and visual rendering. Document animation serialization, playback synchronization, and debugging approaches for complex animation sequences.

wiki_catalogs.name: Plugin System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Plugin System that enables extensibility and customization of the editor engine. Document the plugin architecture design, plugin registration mechanism through engine.use(), extension points for components, panels, commands, and shortcuts. Explain the standardized plugin interface, lifecycle management, and integration patterns with the core engine. Include examples of creating custom plugins for new element types, additional toolbar panels, and specialized commands. Address plugin dependency management, version compatibility, and best practices for plugin development. Document the relationship between plugins and the command pattern system, ensuring plugins can participate in undo/redo functionality and maintain consistency with the overall editor architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build and Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Build and Development processes including Vite configuration, TypeScript setup, and ESLint configuration. Document the development workflow, build optimization strategies, and production deployment considerations. Explain the multi-project TypeScript setup with app/node references, ESLint rules for React hooks and TypeScript, and how to configure the development environment for optimal performance. Include examples of customizing Vite plugins, optimizing bundle sizes, and setting up hot module replacement. Address debugging techniques, performance profiling, and troubleshooting common build issues. Document the relationship between configuration files and the overall project architecture, ensuring developers can effectively modify and extend the build system.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# AI Editor Engine - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
The AI Editor Engine is a sophisticated **Slides Editor (课件编辑器)** designed as a **lightweight design tool engine**. This project represents a paradigm shift from traditional page editors to a comprehensive design tool framework that separates concerns between data modeling, rendering abstraction, and interactive capabilities.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Data-Driven Architecture**: Implement a Scene Graph-based system where all operations modify underlying data structures rather than directly manipulating DOM
- **Framework Agnostic Design**: Maintain separation between the engine core and UI framework, enabling future renderer switching
- **Animation-Centric Workflow**: Build a Timeline-driven animation system that operates independently of React lifecycle
- **Professional Design Tool Features**: Support advanced capabilities like grouping, layer management, and complex animations

### Target Audience
- Frontend developers seeking to understand modern editor architecture patterns
- Students learning about design tool engineering and component separation
- Teams building collaborative editing solutions with extensible plugin systems

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
The project follows a **layered architecture** with clear separation of concerns:

```mermaid
graph TD
    UI[React UI Layer] --> Engine[Engine Core]
    Engine --> Renderer[Renderer Layer]
    Engine --> Store[Store Layer]
    Engine --> Types[Type Definitions]
    
    subgraph "Engine Core"
        Engine --> Scene[Scene Graph]
        Engine --> Commands[Command System]
        Engine --> Timeline[Timeline Engine]
        Engine --> History[History Management]
    end
    
    subgraph "Renderer Layer"
        Renderer --> DOM[DOM Renderer]
        Renderer --> Canvas[Canvas Renderer (Future)]
    end
    
    subgraph "Store Layer"
        Store --> Zustand[Zustand State Management]
        Store --> EditorState[Editor State]
    end
```

### Design Patterns
- **Command Pattern**: All state changes must go through `engine.execute(command)` for undo/redo support
- **Observer Pattern**: Timeline system drives animations through time-based updates
- **Factory Pattern**: `createEngine()` provides engine instantiation
- **Plugin Architecture**: Extensible system for adding new components and functionality

### System Relationships
The architecture enforces strict boundaries:
- **Engine Layer**: Framework-agnostic core logic
- **Renderer Layer**: Pure data-to-UI transformation
- **Store Layer**: Editor state management separate from scene data
- **UI Layer**: React components that only handle presentation

### Data Flow Diagrams

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant UI as React Component
    participant Engine as Engine Core
    participant Scene as Scene Graph
    participant Renderer as Renderer
    participant DOM as DOM
    
    User->>UI: Drag Operation
    UI->>Engine: engine.execute(MoveCommand)
    Engine->>Scene: Update Element Position
    Scene->>Renderer: Trigger Render
    Renderer->>DOM: Update DOM Elements
    DOM-->>User: Visual Feedback
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Application Bootstrap**: `src/main.tsx` - React root initialization
- **Root Component**: `src/App.tsx` - Main application container with header and canvas layout
- **Canvas Component**: `src/components/Canvas.tsx` - Central editing area with responsive design

### Core Modules
- **Engine Core**: `src/engine/index.ts` - Framework-agnostic core logic and command execution
- **Renderer Layer**: `src/renderer/index.ts` - Pure data-to-UI transformation utilities
- **State Management**: `src/store/index.ts` - Editor state separate from scene data
- **Type Definitions**: `src/types/index.ts` - Shared TypeScript interfaces across the project

### Configuration Approach
- **Build System**: `vite.config.ts` - React plugin configuration with Vite bundler
- **TypeScript Setup**: `tsconfig.json` - Multi-project configuration with app/node references
- **Linting**: `eslint.config.js` - TypeScript-focused linting rules with React hooks enforcement

### External Dependencies
- **Runtime Dependencies**: 
  - `react`: ^18.3.1 - Core React library
  - `react-dom`: ^18.3.1 - React DOM integration
- **Development Dependencies**:
  - `@vitejs/plugin-react`: ^4.3.1 - Vite React plugin
  - `typescript`: ^5.5.3 - Type checking and compilation
  - `eslint`: ^9.9.0 - Code quality enforcement

### Integration Points
- **Drag & Transform**: Integrated with `react-moveable` for element manipulation
- **State Management**: Uses Zustand for lightweight state management
- **Animation System**: Implements custom Timeline engine with requestAnimationFrame
- **Path Animation**: SVG-based path editing with control point manipulation

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class Engine {
        +execute(command)
        +undo()
        +redo()
        +scene: Scene
        +history: History
        +timeline: Timeline
    }
    
    class Scene {
        +addElement(element)
        +updateElement(id, data)
        +deleteElement(id)
        +getElement(id)
        +getSlideElements(slideId)
    }
    
    class Renderer {
        +renderElement(element, engine)
        +applyTransform(element)
    }
    
    class Store {
        +editorState: EditorState
        +selectedElement: string
        +zoomLevel: number
    }
    
    Engine --> Scene
    Engine --> Renderer
    Engine --> Store
    Renderer --> Scene
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
The project implements a comprehensive set of features designed for professional slide creation:

#### Core Editing Capabilities
- **Multi-page Support**: Document with multiple slides (Document → Slide → Element hierarchy)
- **Visual Editing**: Drag-and-drop, resize, rotate operations with precise positioning
- **Basic Components**: Shape, image, text, and group elements with hierarchical relationships
- **Group Management**: Nested grouping with relative coordinate systems

#### Advanced Systems
- **Animation Engine**: Timeline-based animation system supporting enter/exit and emphasis animations
- **Keyframe System**: Precise animation control with easing curves and timing
- **Time-based Rendering**: requestAnimationFrame-driven animation playback
- **Path Animation**: SVG path-based movement with control point editing

#### Professional Features
- **Layer Management**: Tree-based hierarchy with drag-and-drop reordering
- **Z-index Control**: Visual layer ordering independent of DOM structure
- **Snap System**: Intelligent alignment with grid-based snapping
- **Undo/Redo**: Complete command history with proper state restoration

### Implementation Highlights

#### Scene Graph Architecture
The core data model follows a hierarchical structure:

```mermaid
erDiagram
    DOCUMENT ||--o{ SLIDE : contains
    SLIDE ||--o{ ELEMENT : contains
    ELEMENT ||--o{ ANIMATION : has
    ANIMATION ||--o{ KEYFRAME : contains
    
    DOCUMENT {
        string id
        array nodes
        array slides
    }
    
    SLIDE {
        string id
        string name
        array elementIds
    }
    
    ELEMENT {
        string id
        string type
        string parentId
        array children
        number x
        number y
        number width
        number height
        number rotation
        number zIndex
    }
    
    ANIMATION {
        string id
        string type
        number start
        number duration
        string easing
    }
    
    KEYFRAME {
        number offset
        object props
    }
```

#### Timeline Animation System
The animation engine operates independently of React components:

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle
    Idle --> Playing: play()
    Playing --> Paused: pause()
    Paused --> Playing: play()
    Playing --> Seeking: seek()
    Seeking --> Playing: continue
    Playing --> Completed: animationEnd
    Completed --> Idle: reset()
    
    state Playing {
        [*] --> Calculating
        Calculating --> Interpolating: keyframeCalculation
        Interpolating --> Updating: applyTransform
        Updating --> Calculating: nextFrame
    }
```

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    UI[React Components] --> Interaction[Interaction Layer]
    Interaction --> Engine[Engine.execute]
    Engine --> Command[Command Pattern]
    Command --> Scene[Scene Graph Update]
    Scene --> Renderer[Renderer]
    Renderer --> DOM[DOM Update]
    
    subgraph "Animation Pipeline"
        Timeline[Timeline Engine] --> Interpolator[Keyframe Interpolator]
        Interpolator --> Transform[Transform Application]
        Transform --> Renderer
    end
    
    subgraph "State Management"
        Store[Editor State] --> Selection[Selection State]
        Store --> Zoom[Zoom State]
        Store --> Tools[Tool State]
    end
```

## 5. Conclusion and Recommendations

### Project Architecture Summary
The AI Editor Engine demonstrates exceptional architectural design with clear separation of concerns. The project successfully implements a **framework-agnostic engine core** that maintains complete control over state while providing flexible rendering options. The **Scene Graph architecture** serves as the single source of truth, enabling powerful features like undo/redo, collaborative editing, and animation systems.

### Key Architectural Strengths
- **Clean Separation**: Engine, renderer, and UI layers maintain strict boundaries
- **Data-First Approach**: All modifications go through the engine, ensuring consistency
- **Extensibility**: Plugin system and abstract renderer design enable future enhancements
- **Professional Features**: Advanced capabilities like timeline animation and snap system
- **Type Safety**: Comprehensive TypeScript implementation with strict mode enforcement

### Potential Improvement Areas
- **Testing Coverage**: Consider adding unit tests for the engine and command system
- **Performance Optimization**: Implement virtual scrolling for large documents
- **Accessibility**: Add ARIA labels and keyboard navigation support
- **Documentation**: Expand inline documentation for the plugin system
- **Error Handling**: Implement robust error boundaries in React components

### Actionable Recommendations
1. **Implement Comprehensive Testing**: Add Jest/React Testing Library for engine and component testing
2. **Performance Monitoring**: Integrate React Profiler and performance metrics collection
3. **Plugin Registry**: Develop a standardized plugin registration system
4. **Collaborative Features**: Implement Yjs-based CRDT for real-time collaboration
5. **Export System**: Add support for exporting to various formats (PDF, PNG, HTML)

### Next Steps for Project Evolution
- **Phase 1 Completion**: Focus on stabilizing the current MVP with full CRUD operations
- **Phase 2 Enhancement**: Implement advanced snapping and alignment features
- **Phase 3 Expansion**: Add export capabilities and format support
- **Phase 4 Platformization**: Develop plugin ecosystem and marketplace
- **Phase 5 Enterprise**: Add team collaboration and admin features

This project represents a mature approach to modern editor architecture, providing both educational value and practical foundation for building professional design tools.

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [main.tsx](src/main.tsx)
- [App.tsx](src/App.tsx)
- [Canvas.tsx](src/components/Canvas.tsx)
- [engine/index.ts](src/engine/index.ts)
- [renderer/index.ts](src/renderer/index.ts)
- [store/index.ts](src/store/index.ts)
- [types/index.ts](src/types/index.ts)
- [vite.config.ts](vite.config.ts)
- [tsconfig.json](tsconfig.json)
- [tsconfig.app.json](tsconfig.app.json)
- [spec.md](spec.md)
- [spec1.md](spec1.md)
- [eslint.config.js](eslint.config.js)
- [index.html](index.html)

</blog>
